title: Incident Of The French Camp
content:
I.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ You know, we French stormed Ratisbon:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A mile or so away,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ On a little mound, Napolן¿½on
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Stood on our storming-day;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ With neck out-thrust, you fancy how,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Legs wide, arms locked behind,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ As if to balance the prone brow
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oppressive with its mind.

II.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Just as perhaps he mused ן¿½My plans
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½That soar, to earth may fall,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Let once my army-leader Lannes
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Waver at yonder wall,ן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Out ן¿½twixt the battery-smokes there flew
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A rider, bound on bound
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Full-galloping; nor bridle drew
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Until he reached the mound.

III.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Then off there flung in smiling joy,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And held himself erect
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ By just his horseן¿½s mane, a boy:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ You hardly could suspect
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ (So tight he kept his lips compressed,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Scarce any blood came through)
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ You looked twice ere you saw his breast
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Was all but shot in two.

IV.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Well,ן¿½ cried he, ן¿½Emperor, by Godן¿½s grace
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Weן¿½ve got you Ratisbon!
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½The Marshalן¿½s in the market-place,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½And youן¿½ll be there anon
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½To see your flag-bird flap his vans
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Where I, to heartן¿½s desire,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Perched him!ן¿½ The chiefן¿½s eye flashed; his plans
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Soared up again like fire.

V.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The chiefן¿½s eye flashed; but presently
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Softened itself, as sheathes
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ A film the mother-eagleן¿½s eye
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ When her bruised eaglet breathes;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Youן¿½re wounded!ן¿½ ן¿½Nay,ן¿½ the soldierן¿½s pride
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Touched to the quick, he said:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ן¿½Iן¿½m killed, Sire!ן¿½ And his chief beside
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Smiling the boy fell dead.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Browning represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Robert Browning's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
